---
title: |
  Materials Genomics<br>Unit 6: Uncertainty and Error Bars in Materials ML
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Materials Genomics"
---

## 01. Title: Uncertainty and Error Bars in Materials ML
- Unit role in MG flow.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Quantify and communicate uncertainty.

## 03. Recap from Unit 5
- Supervised models and residuals.

## 04. Why uncertainty matters in discovery
- Decisions under risk.

## 05. Aleatoric vs epistemic uncertainty
- Two uncertainty sources.

## 06. Measurement uncertainty in materials data
- Instrument and protocol effects.

## 07. Label noise and weak supervision
- Consequences for confidence.

## 08. Data scarcity and epistemic risk
- Extrapolation danger.

## 09. Error bars basics
- What they represent.

## 10. Confidence vs prediction intervals
- Parameter vs outcome uncertainty.

## 11. Calibration fundamentals
- Reliability diagrams intuition.

## 12. Miscalibration patterns
- Over/underconfidence behavior.

## 13. Quantile regression idea
- Direct interval prediction.

## 14. Heteroscedastic modeling
- Input-dependent noise.

## 15. Ensemble uncertainty
- Diversity-based confidence.

## 16. MC dropout concept
- Approximate Bayesian view.

## 17. Bayesian linear model preview
- Posterior uncertainty intuition.

## 18. Conformal prediction concept
- Distribution-free coverage.

## 19. OOD detection cues
- Shift and novelty signals.

## 20. Domain shift in materials datasets
- Cross-database risk.

## 21. Uncertainty-aware ranking
- Candidate prioritization logic.

## 22. Acquisition with uncertainty
- Active learning bridge.

## 23. Cost-aware decision thresholds
- Utility framing.

## 24. Error decomposition by subgroup
- Chemistry/structure slices.

## 25. Coverage diagnostics
- Empirical interval checks.

## 26. Sharpness vs calibration tradeoff
- Useful uncertainty quality.

## 27. Benchmarking uncertainty methods
- Fair comparison setup.

## 28. Reporting standards
- Include uncertainty metadata.

## 29. Visualization patterns
- Fan charts and interval plots.

## 30. Communication to domain experts
- Interpretable confidence statements.

## 31. Case sketch: bandgap with intervals
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 32. Case sketch: stability classification confidence
- Risk-aware triage.

## 33. Case sketch: noisy synthesis labels
- Robust handling strategy.

## 34. Failure mode #1
- Confidently wrong predictions.

## 35. Failure mode #2
- Uncertainty ignored in selection.

## 36. Failure mode #3
- Leakage inflating confidence.

## 37. Mitigation checklist
- Practical safeguards.

## 38. Uncertainty in optimization loops
- Bridge to inverse design.

## 39. Reproducibility requirements
- Seeds, folds, and logs.

## 40. Model card uncertainty section
- Documentation template.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: estimate intervals
- Train + interval outputs.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: calibration analysis
- Reliability + coverage plots.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: subgroup uncertainty
- Slice-based diagnostics.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: decision policy
- Threshold by uncertainty.

## 45. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield content.

## 46. Summary table
- Methods vs assumptions.

## 47. Practical deployment notes
- Human review triggers.

## 48. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 49. Reading assignment
- Prepare active learning unit.

## 50. References + transition
- Link to model interpretation.

::: {#refs}
:::
